transcript.Question: Is there a post office nearby?

transcript.Question: When will the next interview begin?

transcript.B: I met with him this

transcript.Question: Should we give our new recruits the test on company products before or after lunch?

introduction: Questions 32 through 34 refer to the following conversation.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Welcome to the WRO charitable fundraiser. Please check the seating plan here. Can I take a note of the company you work? For sure I'm the founder of Delta Digital, a leading developer of business software. I'm here to give a speech and make a sizable donation to your organization. Oh, we are so happy to have you here. Mister Reid. I have a special VIP welcome packet for you here. It includes some beverage vouchers and an event program.

introduction: Questions 35 through 37 refer to the following conversation.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Hello I dropped my mobile phone a few days ago and the screen is cracked. A friend of mine recommended bringing it here to be fixed. Can that be done without an appointment? Yes but I won't be able to look at it until later today. Can you leave the device with me for a few hours? Oh, but I'll need my phone this afternoon. I have to drive out of town for a job interview and I'll need to use the GPs on my phone. I understand well. If you can bring it in at 9 o'clock to Morrow morning, I'll be able to fix it right away.

introduction: Questions 38 through 40 refer to the following conversation.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Hi Rashida, our supplier just dropped off the new uniforms for your employees. I'm going to bring them over to your branch this afternoon. Thanks Rob and we need to make sure the old uniforms are recycled. It would be very wasteful to just throw them away. Could you find out what our options are? No problem since the old shirts and pants don't feature our company branding, they could maybe be used by other people. Exactly. We could even donate them to a homeless shelter. The 1 on Wells Street might be interested. 1 of us should ask them. Well, I'll be passing Wells Street this afternoon. It's on my way to your branch. That would be ideal.

introduction: Questions 41 through 43 refer to the following conversation with three speakers.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Debby Calvin I'd like to hear your ideas for improving staff morale in our workplace. Well, it's best that we listen to Kelvin's ideas. 1 Since he was successful in motivating staff at his previous job. Well, I feel the best way to boost employee morale is to set targets and rewards. For instance, at this company, we could reward employees who sell a certain number of sofas or dining tables. Great idea. Why don't we start by looking at the sales figures for our 3 seater sofas then we can set a target for staff and announce an attractive bonus.

introduction: Questions 44 through 46 refer to the following conversation.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Hi, welcome to Lotus Garden. We are excited to launch our new and improved menu this evening. Do you have a reservation? Yes it's under the name Sandra Potter and I booked a table for 4 at 7 PM. Hi Miss Potter, I have good news for you. To celebrate the start of our new menu, we're offering 50 percent of our appetizers and desserts. Wow I noticed your squid starter on social media and I'm tempted but I don't know. I've never tried squid before. Well, we let our diners try samples of our dishes before ordering. I can ask the chef to bring 1 to your table.

introduction: Questions 47 through 49 refer to the following conversation.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Hm. I'm afraid your driving license is only for automatic vehicles. You need to have a license for manual cars. Oh, but I only have 1 for automatic vehicles. That's why I hired a car with automatic transmission. You rented a manual car through our website. Really I asked my assistant to make the booking. She may have made a mistake. Well, is there anything you can do for me? We have another branch at the bus terminal downtown and they have automatic vehicles available. I'll transfer your booking over and you can take a taxi there.

introduction: Questions 50 through 52 refer to the following conversation.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Hi Katy Before we open the agency today, I'm going to add these 15 new properties to our website. I've taken photographs of all the rooms. Okay but we still need to hire an experienced company to start taking pictures of our homes. You're doing a good job but professional photos would look. I know I've already contacted Image Experts, a business that specializes in taking pictures of property. The owner of the business is coming here to meet me at 11 A M. Today. Great I'll join you for that. I want to make sure they offer reasonable rates for their services.

introduction: Questions 53 through 55 refer to the following conversation with 3 speakers.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Thanks for coming out to our company headquarters for this meeting. Yes thank you, Miss Ulson. We really hope you can show us some strategies to make our health food products more visible to potential customers on the Internet. I'm happy to be here. There are several ways to market products online. What did you have in? Well, we've been considering working with popular social media influencers and having them endorse our products. We looked into it though and many of them request high fees. Well, 1 way to lower costs is to provide them with free products or a lifetime discount. Of course, that means you'd need to work with an individual who genuinely likes your products.

introduction: Questions 56 through 58 refer to the following conversation.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Hi, Maria Can I have a quick chat with you? Of course, is everything okay? Well, I just came out of the board meeting and we have decided to relocate our company to a larger workspace in the downtown area. Oh really Yes we'll be hiring more staff and taking on new clients so we need more space. It means we'll probably close the office this Friday while we move. Okay but that's the final day of our pay period. If we close, I might not be able to hit my sales target. I understand we are going to lower the target this month to help all staff so just work as hard as you can to reach it by Thursday.

introduction: Questions 59 through 61 refer to the following conversation.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Good afternoon my name is EVA and I'm the Storem manager. Can I help you with anything? Yes I recently started working at a local kindergarten and I want to buy some art supplies to use in class. A friend recommended Art Hub Supplies but I can't find that brand anywhere. A lot of people have been asking for that brand but you can only buy those items directly through the company's website. They aren't stocked by retailers. Oh, that's a little disappointing To be honest we have lots of products that are at least as good as those items and more affordably priced. Well, I was hoping to get supplies today so I'd be interested. Great I'd be happy to recommend some to you right now.

introduction: Questions 71 through 73 refer to the following excerpt from a meeting.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: As you all know, we have found it difficult to break into foreign markets with our clothing ranges. I heard that another sportswear manufacturer overcame this issue by taking on an innovative strategy. They contacted famous sports celebrities in foreign markets and paid them to endorse their products and it had a significant impact on sales. If we had endorsements from a few famous athletes, we could boost our brand recognition overseas. This would be a new approach for us. So I've invited Pamela Shepherd from Global Consulting to our next meeting. Miss Shepherd provides expert advice to businesses on how to market products overseas.

introduction: Questions 74 through 76 refer to the following BroadCast Next on WKRO Radio, we have your local news report this morning.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: I'm at Magnolia Park in downtown San Diego, where the Mega Music Festival is due to take place over the next few days. The event was scheduled to begin at 10 M but event organizers have been forced to push back the start time by 3 hours due to heavy rain and strong winds. Thousands of festival goers are waiting in line and many have decided just to go. If there is no improvement in the conditions, the organizers may have to provide ticket refunds. Anyone planning to attend the event can check the event website for regular updates on the situation.

introduction: Questions 77 through 79 refer to the following talk.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: It's my pleasure to welcome you all to this online course hosted by Grizzwald Business Institute. I'm Richard Wright and I'll be teaching you how to budget effectively and save money on your household bills, grocery shopping and other things. As you know, this course differs from other similar courses in that it costs nothing for you to join. The only thing we ask is that you make a donation to 1 of our partner charities. If you feel the course has been beneficial to you. Now I'm going to show you some slides that outline the structure of this 4 week course. Please mute your microphones for a few minutes.

introduction: Questions 80 through 82 refer to the following excerpt from a meeting.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: But please don't forget that our cinema is hosting the premier of the new Tom Wells movie next Saturday. That means we only have 9 days to make sure everything is prepared. Each of you will be given special formal wear which you must wear that evening. We already have your measurements and you should pick up your suit or dress from the admin office next Tuesday. Many of you have requested a ticket for a friend or family member to attend the event but there is a limited number of tickets. I can't make any promises.

introduction: Questions 83 through 85 refer to the following tour information.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Hi, everyone and welcome to the 3 stop on the tour Petruchy Fountain. I hope you all brought the vouchers I handed out to you on the tour bus. Those can be used to get 50 percent off pizza and gelaatto at a nearby restaurant. After we view the fountain. 1 I'm going to tell you about the interesting method used to sculpt the statues surrounding the fountain. It's hard to believe, but all these statues were carved from 1 huge block of stone in the 1600s. The fountain's condition had deteriorated over the years, but work was completed last month to restore it to its original condition.

introduction: Questions 86 through 88 refer to the following excerpt from a meeting.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: It's a pleasure to meet you all this morning. As some of you may know, my name is Tina Moore and I'm the new health and Well being officer here at the manufacturing plant. My job is to ensure that all employees are happy and coping well with the challenges of their jobs. Over the next few weeks, I will be scheduling counseling sessions for each of you in my office at 6 PM. However, if you have normally left for home by that time, the timetable is somewhat flexible. Don't forget that you can reach out to me at any time at Tina Moore at Erdogo Com. All messages will be treated as private and confidential.

introduction: Questions 89 through 91 refer to the following telephone message.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Hi BRT We have a slight problem with the software conference this weekend. The person who is supposed to deliver the keynote speech will not be able to attend the event. Mile Stanfort, the CEO of Stanfort Software, has had to pull out at the last minute because he needs to fly to Australia for a family emergency. We cannot make any changes to the schedule as it has already been posted on our website and sent out to all event attendees. So I need you to think of some individuals who may be willing to take Mister Stanford's place and get in contact with them.

introduction: Questions 92 through 94 refer to the following talk.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: Good afternoon Every 1 I hope you are all enjoying the Active Sports Convention. As the lead product designer at Alpine Pursuits, it is my pleasure to introduce our brand new Model of tent, the Maverick 750. Among all the tents we have produced, we are confident that the Maverick 7 50 will be our most popular yet. 1 thing that makes this Model truly innovative is that it can be completely reversed. This means that you can choose between 2 different colors depending on which 1 you prefer. We have brought along some tents that can change from green to blue and you can get 1 for free to day by completing a survey. Just keep in mind, we could only fit 50 in our booth so head on over after my presentation if you're interested.

introduction: Questions 95 through 97 refer to the following talk and floor plan.

transcript.speaker: Speaker
transcript.text: I'd like all waiting staff to know that we are welcoming a very special guest this Sunday. Margaret Burrows has been coming to our restaurant regularly since it opened 30 years ago and she will be celebrating her 100th birthday here on Sunday. The party has been arranged by her daughter who has requested the private dining room with 2 round tables. I want all of you to attend to the needs of the guests and make this a truly memorable occasion for everyone. When the guests arrive, remember to offer them a complimentary glass of sparkling wine or a soft drink. And if you have any questions, please let me know.
